studies.preview: Persistent occlusive DVT in the right femoral and posterior tibial veins. No new left-leg DVT. Compared with 7/6 — largely unchanged residual right-leg thrombus.

NEW right ventricular dilation and hypokinesis with septal flattening (McConnell-pattern). Elevated estimated RV systolic pressure. Findings of acute RV strain — NOT present on the 7/6 study. Consistent with intermediate-risk (submassive) PE.
PROGRESSED pulmonary embolism — now involving BOTH main pulmonary arteries (bilateral), advanced beyond the segmental clot on the 7/6 CTPA. RV:LV ratio >1.0 suggesting strain. No frank infarction.

Large right-hepatic-lobe lesion with heterogeneous enhancement; NON-DIAGNOSTIC for a specific etiology but concerning for malignancy. Does not exclude primary vs metastatic disease. Tissue sampling recommended.

studies.description: MRI Liver with and without contrast
studies.preview: A 17 cm abnormality in the posterior right hepatic lobe, additional abnormal-signal foci in the left lobe, and apparent THROMBUS within branches of the right portal vein. HIGH SUSPICION for malignancy (primary vs metastatic). Recommendation: consider needle biopsy.
Normal right ventricular size and function. NO evidence of right-heart strain. Normal LV function. Establishes a low-risk PE at presentation (baseline for later comparison).
Occlusive DVT involving the right proximal (common/proximal) femoral vein, posterior tibial veins, and greater saphenous vein. Extensive right-leg clot burden.

studies.description: CT Pulmonary Angiogram (with incidental liver finding)
studies.preview: SEGMENTAL pulmonary embolus (no central/saddle clot; RV:LV ratio normal). Incidental heterogeneous enhancement in the right hepatic lobe — favored to represent focal fatty infiltration, but the differential includes primary or metastatic malignancy; dedicated liver imaging recommended.
